questions.question: How do elections strengthen democracy?
questions.answer: They allow citizens to choose and replace their representatives.

questions.question: Why did the Constitution makers adopt political competition?
questions.answer: To ensure leaders remain accountable to the people.

questions.question: What principle ensures each vote has equal value?
questions.answer: The principle of one person, one vote, one value.

questions.question: Why must constituencies have roughly equal populations?
questions.answer: To ensure equal representation in elections.

questions.question: What is delimitation?
questions.answer: Delimitation is the process of fixing boundaries of constituencies.

questions.question: Why is the voters list revised regularly?
questions.answer: To include new voters and remove names of deceased or migrated people.

questions.question: Why must candidates file nomination forms?
questions.answer: To officially contest elections.

questions.question: Why must candidates disclose criminal records?
questions.answer: To inform voters and ensure transparency.

questions.question: What role do polling agents play?
questions.answer: They ensure voting is conducted fairly at polling booths.

questions.question: Why are EVMs sealed after polling?
questions.answer: To prevent tampering before counting.

questions.question: What ensures independence of the Election Commission?
questions.answer: Its constitutional status and protection from government interference.

questions.question: Why can the Election Commission order repolling?
questions.answer: To ensure fairness if irregularities occur.

questions.question: Why is high voter turnout important in democracy?
questions.answer: It reflects active public participation.

questions.question: What challenge does money power create in elections?
questions.answer: It gives wealthy candidates an unfair advantage.

questions.question: What problem arises from criminal candidates in elections?
questions.answer: They may misuse power and undermine democracy.

questions.question: Why do some families dominate political parties?
questions.answer: Because leadership positions are often passed to relatives.

questions.question: Why is electoral reform discussed in India?
questions.answer: To improve fairness and transparency in elections.

questions.question: What does acceptance of election results indicate?
questions.answer: That elections are fair and democratic.

questions.question: Why are elections sometimes called a political exam?
questions.answer: Because voters judge leaders’ performance through voting.

questions.question: What role do voters play in a democracy?
questions.answer: They select leaders and influence government policies.

questions.question: Why is secret ballot important?
questions.answer: It allows voters to vote freely without fear.

questions.question: Why do elections take place at regular intervals?
questions.answer: To maintain accountability of the government.

questions.question: What ensures that elections remain competitive?
questions.answer: Multiple parties and candidates contesting elections.

questions.question: Why is transparency important in elections?
questions.answer: It builds public trust in democratic processes.

questions.question: What is the ultimate aim of democratic elections?
questions.answer: To reflect the will of the people.
